# sf-deck v0.1.2

This patch makes sf-deck's local-first privacy boundary explicit and adds the
controls needed to inspect, erase, and revoke access before launch.

## Privacy and authorization

- A versioned acknowledgement is shown before sf-deck first discovers or
  contacts a real Salesforce org.
- sf-deck now publishes a plain-language privacy notice and user agreement,
  linked from the TUI, website, README, and documentation.
- The notice records the actual architecture: sf-deck has no hosted backend,
  account system, application telemetry, or central customer-data store.
- Record lists/details, SOQL/report rows, list-view results, related-record
  lookups, and Salesforce RecentlyViewed rows stay in process memory. Saved
  query text/history and metadata caches remain local working state.

## Local-data controls

```sh
sf-deck data inspect
sf-deck data erase --yes
sf-deck org logout --org <alias-or-username> --yes
```

Erasure refuses to run while another sf-deck instance is active. It does not
remove Salesforce CLI credentials, custom export paths, or custom bundle paths;
those boundaries are reported directly in the command output and docs.

Headless users can review and acknowledge the current policy revision with:

```sh
sf-deck legal status
sf-deck legal accept --yes
```

Demo mode remains fully offline and does not require acknowledgement.

## Install or upgrade

```sh
brew upgrade --cask Jacob-Stokes/tap/sf-deck
```

New installations:

```sh
brew install --cask Jacob-Stokes/tap/sf-deck
```
